negative_variants.description: Packed Logic Bus Slice: keeps the file compilable but forces the observable output path low, so it can pass include/instance smoke checks while failing the required behavior to y[3:2] must follow a[7:6].

Packed Logic Bus Slice: keeps the main operator syntax but collapses the computed value through the wrong condition, so coarse smoke cases may run while boundary and waveform checks for Use a packed-style logic bus with slices and concatenation fail.
Packed Logic Bus Slice: introduces incorrect initialization or state bias, so startup and reset-sensitive checks fail while the model still compiles and produces activity.
Packed Logic Bus Slice: offsets the secondary metric or observable path, so the primary waveform may look plausible while calibrated checker evidence for y[3:2] must follow a[7:6] fails.

Packed Logic Bus Slice: scales the final observable response, so compile and rough activity checks can pass while calibrated output checks for Use a packed-style logic bus with slices and concatenation fail.
